title: Austin Places — bars, hikes, hotpot, sushi, jazz, taxidermy museum
html:
Austin Places — bars, hikes, hotpot, sushi, jazz, taxidermy museum

Source: 2025-11-01 Apple Note. Yahir's running Austin hit-list — what looks like the post-arrival exploration ledger that grows for 6+ months (last modified April 2026).

TL;DR

  • Bars: Equipment Room, Redhead Stepchild (with code), Kelly Irish Pub (St. Paddy's), Tiger Lilly (Friday Jazz + half-off martinis), Latchkeh, Vigilante, Mean Eyed Cat, Live Oak Brewery, Continental Club.
  • Asian food: Asia Cafe, 888 Vietnamese, Old Alley Hotpot, Pho Dan, Khaki Man Ghai, Man Man, Ho Ho.
  • Sushi: Sushi Warriors (AYCE $39.99), Seoul Sushi, Sushi Junai.
  • Hikes/nature: Hamilton Springs, Mount Bonnell, Jessica Hollis Park, The Lookout, Hippie Hollow, Krause Springs, Steel Bridge, Hill of Life Dam of Life, Aquodome, Enchanted Rock.
  • Coffee/breakfast: A Hole Coffee, Phoebe, Snooze AM, Bird Bird Biscuit, Joe Bakery, Cosmic Cafe.
  • Tex-Mex: Cuantos Tacos, Guadalajara Burnet, Aguirre Tacos.
  • Curiosities: Roaring Hunt Taxidermy Museum, Mean Eyed Cat Bar, Halcyon, Moonshine.
  • Live music: Seaboys, Continental Club, Tiger Lilly Friday jazz.
  • Two business-meta lines tacked on bottom: "more intentional outreach" / "why wouldn't they do this in house??" — Rankett-mode bleeding into the food-list note.

Pattern observed

This is Yahir's living recommendation engine for Austin — a personal Yelp shadow-database. Same format as the Shanghai coffee shops list from the China chapter — geographic-curiosity-as-list. Subset of these places appear in the visit planning doc as the curated short-list for Emily.

Open questions

  • Which of these has Yahir actually been to? (Probably most of the bars + breakfast spots.)
  • Is the "Roaring Hunt Taxidermy Museum" a real place? (Yes — likely Roaring Hunts taxidermy.)
  • Why hotpot (Old Alley) — Chinese-import nostalgia from Shanghai?
